01

Core Strengths

I.

GTM Strategy & Demand Generation

From market entry to pipeline acceleration — builds go-to-market strategies from scratch, aligns sales and marketing around shared revenue goals, and executes campaigns that convert.

II.

Brand Architecture & Visual Identity

Rare combination of strategic positioning and hands-on design capability. Has launched brands for startups, rebranded established companies, and created full visual identity systems from logo to collateral.

III.

Cross-Cultural Team Leadership

Has led multicultural teams across Egypt, Germany, and remote-first setups. German University graduate with operational experience in the German market and business-fluent English.

IV.

Project & Resource Management

Managed simultaneous portfolios of 5+ client accounts using agile methodologies. Experienced with Asana, HubSpot, Stackfield — strong on timelines, deliverables, and stakeholder communication.

V.

Digital Marketing & Analytics

Performance-oriented approach: LinkedIn Ads, Meta Ads, SEO, email marketing, and CRM. Consistently connects campaign metrics to business KPIs rather than vanity numbers.

VI.

UX & Product Marketing

Led end-to-end product launches with UX design, platform messaging, and user flow optimization. Applied product marketing thinking to SaaS, e-learning, and B2B services.

03

B2B Marketing in Action

Case Study · Demand Generation

From Invisible
to In-Demand

Reactivating Dale Carnegie Egypt's brand presence and rebuilding its lead generation engine from the ground up — as a solo remote marketing manager over 9 months.

+23%Lead Growth
+30%Social Followers
+25%Email Open Rate
7Trainings Delivered

01 · The Situation

A brand that had gone dark.

People thought the franchise had closed. The brand was almost non-existent online — outdated identity, near-zero marketing activity, and an underperforming lead pipeline.

Brand AwarenessNear zero
Online PresenceOutdated & inactive
Lead PipelineUnderperforming

02 · The Strategy

A system built to restore trust, generate demand, and support sales.

01Brand Reactivation

Updated global website landing pages for each training. Refreshed positioning to align with Dale Carnegie's global identity.

02Trainer-Led Content

Built a content system centred on individual trainers — bios, learning outcomes, video snippets. Our most original strategic bet.

03Multi-Channel Demand Gen

Monthly email campaigns, quarterly content calendar mixing global and local content, social media posted consistently.

04Sales Enablement

Created 1-pager training outlines for the sales team. Updated company profile with all B2B offerings for corporate clients.

03 · The Original Insight

Make the trainer the campaign.

In professional training, buyers don't buy a program — they buy a person. We built content that put each trainer front and centre across three stages:

1 · Introduce

Image post + short trainer bio

2 · Educate

Carousel: key learning outcomes

3 · Prove

Video snippets from live sessions

Result: Highest engagement came from training recap posts. Attendees commented publicly — organic social proof that money can't buy.

04 · What This Demonstrates

I don't just execute. I diagnose, design and deliver.

Strategic Diagnosis

I didn't inherit a brief — I defined the problem. Mapping customer journeys with sales, identifying the trainer as the real differentiator, choosing channels that made sense for the audience.

End-to-End Execution

Website, email, social, content, sales collateral, event coverage — all owned and run remotely with a lean team. No agency. No large budget. Just clear priorities.

Measurable Impact

Every decision was connected to a business outcome. 23% more leads, 7 booked trainings, a brand that went from invisible to active in under a year.
